chapter_title.ar: بَابُ فَضْلِ الْمُقَامِ بِالْمَدِينَةِ وَ الصَّوْمِ وَ الِاعْتِكَافِ عِنْدَ الْأَسَاطِينِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 219 – Merits of the staying in Al-Medina, and the Fasting, and I’tikaaf (seclusion) by the pillars (of the Masjid of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/270)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (18/270)
text: مَنْ مَاتَ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ بَعَثَهُ اللَّهُ فِي الْآمِنِينَ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ مِنْهُمْ يَحْيَى بْنُ حَبِيبٍ وَ أَبُو عُبَيْدَةَ الْحَذَّاءُ وَ عَبْدُ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنُ الْحَجَّاجِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Isa, from Muhammad Bin Amro Al Zayyat,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who dies in Al-Medina, Allah<sup>azwj</sup> would Resurrect him among the Secured ones on the Day of Judgement – among them are Yahya Bin Habeeb, and Abu Ubeyda Al-Haza’a, and Abdul Rahman Bin Al-Hajjaj’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn ‘Isa from Muhammad ibn ‘Amr al- Zayyat who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that if one dies in al-Madinah, Allah will raise him on the Day of Judgment among those who have received immunity. Of such people are Yahya ibn Habib, abu ‘Ubaydah al-Hadhdha’ and ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj.”
